shape_semantics: cmp,vp,vpc,naive = W,W,W,L. Every CMP-aware technique (cmplog/I2S, value_profile/Hamming, and the vpc combination) resolves the gate; only naive blocks. This is the CMP-SOLVABLE-GATE pattern, NOT a joint-necessity shape: vpc does not need both techniques, since either one alone already resolves (cmplog=W and value_profile=W independently). The label-source is therefore 'any CMP technique enriches/closes the gate target; naive does not'.

hypotheses.label: A fixed-offset literal value gate (FOURCC/tag/enum/single byte) is solved by any CMP-aware fuzzer (I2S substitution or VP Hamming gradient) while naive's blind havoc cannot place the literal

params.comment: Per W/L gate offset, log2 enrichment of the corpus byte == target (W) value vs decoy (L) value, cmplog-and-value_profile vs naive. CMP-aware fuzzers should enrich the target literal at the gate offset; naive should not.
params.target_value: GPOS table tag (47 50 4f 53) at table-directory offsets ~0x0c-0x0f
params.decoy_value: diffuse non-tag bytes at the same offsets in naive loser seeds

hypotheses.prediction: signed_target_enrich >= +1.0 (CMP-aware corpora over-represent the GPOS-tag bytes at the gate offset relative to naive) AND decoy_enrich <= 0 (CMP-aware does not over-represent the off-gate decoy bytes). DISCRIMINATING: a pure structural/length mechanism (not a literal-value gate) would predict signed_target_enrich ~ 0 at any fixed offset because no single byte value is privileged; an enrichment confirms the gate is a fixed-offset literal that CMP introspection targets, ruling out a structural-assembly alternative. G2: enrichment is measured over corpus byte content, independent of the resolve pattern that defined the shape.

hypotheses.label: The CMP-aware corpus (value_profile Hamming gradient / I2S substitution) lands at or near the gate operand literal in a sliding-window distance sense, while naive's corpus stays far from it -- a CLOSENESS signal that survives dispersed/different-sized seeds where fixed-offset byte enrichment fails
hypotheses.novelty_vs_prior: operand_enrichment (byte_freq_ratio) failed (rule_not_met / seed_starved) for these branches because it requires byte-ALIGNED fixed-offset frequency of the EXACT target byte. value_distance_reached is a genuinely different measurement: per-arm MIN over sliding windows of normalized Hamming distance to the operand literal. It (a) captures the VP Hamming-GRADIENT mechanism (h1 in every signature: 'progressively closer' / 'closer-prefix bucket retained') that fixed-offset exact-byte frequency cannot see, and (b) tolerates dispersed/variable-position operands (sliding window, no alignment), so it scores gates that defeated byte_freq_ratio. Not a relaxed-threshold re-run of the prior rule -- a different metric over a different descriptor.

params.comment: Per arm, MIN over the arm's seeds of (MIN over sliding windows of Hamming(window, target_operand)/width). CMP-aware arms (vp/cmp) should land close (small distance); naive should stall far. operand_literal taken per branch from the signature.

libxml2/6967.caveat: width<=2 chance-alignment prone; flagged by tool caveat -- arbiter should honor winner_closer only if distance_gap is non-trivial

libpng/3996.caveat: single-byte color_type enum; width<=2 chance-alignment prone

hypotheses.prediction: winner_closer == true AND distance_gap >= 0.25 -- the CMP-aware arm's corpus reaches a sliding window whose Hamming distance to the operand literal is materially smaller than naive's nearest window. DISCRIMINATING: a NON-CMP mechanism (scheduling / energy / pure structural-length, where naive blocks for a reason unrelated to a value gradient) predicts distance_gap ~ 0 -- naive's corpus would be just as close to (or as far from) the literal as the CMP arms, because nothing is selecting on operand-closeness. A positive gap rules out 'naive blocks for a non-value reason' and confirms a CMP-distance-climbable value gate. G2: distance is computed over raw seed bytes vs a static operand literal, independent of which fuzzer resolved (the resolve pattern is never an input to the distance computation).

hypotheses.label: A non-local grammar/parse-state gate (DTD hash-triple insertion, XML prolog well-formedness chain) whose 'operand' is an abstract multi-token parser state, not a single fixed literal -- I2S repairs and VP gradient-completes dispersed structural tokens across the whole document
hypotheses.decidable_false_reason: No built measurement can discriminate this sub-group's mechanism. (1) operand_literal is null in the signatures: the gate is a parser-STATE predicate (wellFormed==1 chain; a genuinely-new (name,name2,name3) hash triple), not a fixed-offset or even a single sliding-window literal -- so value_distance_reached has no single target operand to measure against, and byte_freq_ratio fails on the different-SIZED dispersed_multibyte seeds (data_reality). (2) joint_necessity / depth_reach target other shapes (assembly-depth vpc-needs-both, ctx/ngram iteration-depth) -- this is a single-technique-suffices CMP gate, not joint, and not a per-seed depth story. (3) libxml2/6405 is additionally seed_starved (W=1, L=27; need >=3 each) and its corpus is on sB only. The mechanism (I2S/VP repairing dispersed structural tokens) is real and likely correct, but it is honestly NON-DISCRIMINABLE with the current tool set -- an honest inconclusive, not a forced label.

data_realities_respected: operand_enrichment (KEEP hypothesis) requires an on-disk corpus AND >=3 W and >=3 L seeds; corpora exist only for curl, harfbuzz, openthread, sqlite3. It scores harfbuzz 5503/5517 (fixed-offset GPOS tag, corpus-backed).

value_distance_reached (NEW hypothesis) reads on-disk seeds, so on-disk-corpus targets only. On s4 it scores harfbuzz 5503/5517 and sqlite3 14451 directly. libxml2/6967 and libpng/3996 are valid designs but their corpora live on sB (libxml2/libpng) -- the OWNING SERVER arbitrates them; they are NOT marked inconclusive merely for being absent on s4 (cross-server rule).

value_distance_reached flags width<=2 operands as chance-alignment-prone: libxml2/6967 ('[' w1) and libpng/3996 (0x03 w1) carry that caveat; the rule's distance_gap>=0.25 floor guards against a chance single-byte alignment passing.

structural_grammar_state_gate (libxml2/6405, 6690) is dispersed_multibyte with operand_literal=null over different-sized seeds: byte_freq_ratio byte-alignment fails (data_reality) AND there is no single operand for value_distance_reached -- so it is honestly decidable:false, NOT force-labeled.

No seed_lineage 'I2S event' or 'VP gradient event' descriptor is proposed: I2S and value_profile are both untagged in mutation_op (I2S is a mutator with byte-identical havoc vocab; VP is a feedback, not a mutator). The only measurable signals are corpus byte content (enrichment) and corpus-to-operand distance (value_distance_reached).

notes.priors_kept: literal_value_gate_cmp_solvable -- kept VERBATIM (rule, descriptor, params unchanged) so the 7 CONFIRMED branches re-validate. Superset/monotonic invariant honored.
notes.new_mechanisms: TWO. (1) cmp_gradient_closes_to_operand (decidable:true) -- pivots from byte_freq_ratio enrichment to value_distance_reached (operand-CLOSENESS), the untried BUILT tool that captures the VP Hamming-gradient + I2S-closeness signal and tolerates dispersed/variable-position operands; targets the rule_not_met branches harfbuzz 5503/5517, sqlite3 14451 (s4 corpus) plus libxml2 6967, libpng 3996 (cross-server, owning server arbitrates). (2) structural_grammar_state_gate (decidable:false) -- the parser-STATE sub-group with null operand that no current tool can discriminate.
notes.why_two_distance_capable_hypotheses_over: harfbuzz 5503/5517 are scored by BOTH the KEEP enrichment hypothesis (fixed-offset GPOS byte frequency) and the NEW distance hypothesis (sliding-window closeness). decision_order tries enrichment FIRST; the distance test is the fallback that ALSO covers them, and crucially extends to sqlite3/libxml2 where the fixed-offset enrichment did not hold (SELECT is a longer/variable-position keyword; '[' is single-byte). They are not redundant -- enrichment is the stronger, more specific test where it applies; distance is the broader net for the rule_not_met set.
notes.decidable_false_subgroup: structural_grammar_state_gate = {libxml2/6405, libxml2/6690}. Honest inconclusive: operand_literal=null parser-state gate, different-sized dispersed seeds, 6405 additionally seed_starved. No built tool discriminates it; carried to fallback=inconclusive per G3.
notes.g1_discrimination: Each decidable hypothesis names the alternative it rules out: enrichment rules out a structural/length gate (~0 fixed-offset enrichment); distance rules out a non-value blocking reason (~0 distance_gap if naive blocks for scheduling/structure rather than an operand gradient). Both are falsifiable and could come out the other way.
notes.g2_independence: Both measurements take raw corpus/seed bytes vs a static target/operand and never consume the W/L resolve pattern that defined the shape -- the verification signal is independent of the label source.
notes.scored_vs_inconclusive_on_s4: S4-SCORED: harfbuzz 5503/5517 (enrichment + distance), sqlite3 14451 (distance). CROSS-SERVER (sB arbitrates): libxml2 6967, libpng 3996 (distance), plus lcms x5 / libpng 3901,3966 / libxml2 -- their owning servers run the same rules on their local corpora. DECIDABLE:FALSE: libxml2 6405, 6690. Remaining confirmed-frozen branches (7x literal_value_gate_cmp_solvable) untouched.
